physics.question: A photon cannot exist at rest and always travels at the same speed in all inertial frames. What fundamental principle enforces this behavior?
Even at absolute zero temperature, particles still possess non-zero energy due to confinement. What is this minimum energy called?
A particle confined in a one-dimensional box cannot have zero energy even in its lowest state. What constraint causes this?
In quantum mechanics, why can probability densities interfere even when individual particles are detected one at a time?
A uniformly accelerated observer detects thermal radiation in vacuum, whereas an inertial observer detects none. What phenomenon explains this?
Black holes emit radiation due to quantum effects near the event horizon. What is this radiation called?
A system evolves deterministically according to Schrödinger equation but appears probabilistic upon measurement. What causes this transition?
In superconductors, electrical resistance drops to zero below a critical temperature due to paired electrons. What are these pairs called?
Why does increasing temperature eventually destroy superconductivity despite increasing thermal energy?
In relativistic mechanics, mass appears to increase with velocity approaching light speed. What underlying factor causes this resistance to acceleration?
A particle moving faster than light in a medium emits a characteristic radiation due to breaking phase velocity limits. What is this radiation?
Why does the entropy of an isolated system never decrease, even though microscopic laws are reversible?
physics.answer: Because systems overwhelmingly evolve toward more probable (higher-multiplicity) states.

physics.question: In quantum field theory, particles are viewed as excitations of underlying fields. What describes these excitations mathematically?
When identical particles are exchanged, the wavefunction may remain same or change sign. What property determines this behavior?
Why do fermions resist occupying the same quantum state, leading to degeneracy pressure in stars?
A rotating frame introduces fictitious forces like Coriolis and centrifugal forces. What is the origin of these forces?
Why does light bend around massive objects even though it has no mass?
In lasers, photons stimulate emission of identical photons, leading to coherent light amplification. What is this process called?
Why does phase difference between waves determine constructive or destructive interference patterns?
In chaotic systems, small changes in initial conditions lead to drastically different outcomes. What phenomenon describes this sensitivity?

In advanced microscopes, electrons are fired toward a metal surface and a measurable current is detected even when the electrons do not have enough classical energy to cross the gap. This surprising observation is best explained by which phenomenon? (A) Photoelectric effect (B) Quantum tunneling (C) Diffraction (D) Interference

Two particles are created together and then separated by a large distance. Measuring one instantly determines the state of the other, regardless of separation. This strange correlation arises due to which concept? (A) Superposition (B) Decoherence (C) Entanglement (D) Refraction

While trying to precisely measure the exact position of an electron using high-energy photons, its momentum becomes highly uncertain. This limitation is fundamentally due to which principle? (A) Pauli exclusion (B) Uncertainty principle (C) Relativity (D) Conservation laws

In a double-slit experiment, when detectors are placed to observe which slit a particle passes through, the interference pattern disappears. This change occurs due to which underlying reason? (A) Energy loss (B) Wavefunction collapse (C) Diffraction limit (D) Polarization

An electron confined within an extremely small region, such as inside a nucleus, tends to escape because confining it increases its kinetic energy drastically. This behavior is explained by which concept? (A) Potential energy drop (B) High kinetic energy (C) Magnetic force (D) Electric shielding

A heat engine operating between two temperatures can never convert all absorbed heat into work, no matter how ideal the design. This fundamental limitation arises due to which law? (A) Zeroth law (B) First law (C) Second law (D) Third law

Light of low intensity but high frequency ejects electrons from a metal surface, while high intensity low-frequency light fails to do so. This observation is explained by which phenomenon? (A) Reflection (B) Photoelectric effect (C) Refraction (D) Scattering

A copper plate moving through a magnetic field experiences a force opposing its motion even without physical contact. This happens due to currents induced inside the conductor. This effect is known as? (A) Hall effect (B) Eddy currents (C) Skin effect (D) Faraday shielding

When alternating current is used for long-distance transmission, energy losses are minimized because voltage can be easily increased and decreased. This advantage is primarily due to what property? (A) Constant resistance (B) Voltage transformation (C) Charge storage (D) Inductance loss

A moving object appears shorter along the direction of motion to a stationary observer. This effect becomes noticeable at speeds close to light. It is explained by which concept? (A) Time dilation (B) Length contraction (C) Doppler shift (D) Redshift

When a charged particle accelerates, it produces oscillating electric and magnetic fields that propagate through space. These fields together form what type of wave? (A) Sound wave (B) Mechanical wave (C) Electromagnetic wave (D) Matter wave

When white light passes through a prism, it splits into different colors because each color bends by a different amount due to variation in refractive index. This phenomenon is called? (A) Reflection (B) Dispersion (C) Diffraction (D) Polarization

A semiconductor becomes more conductive when doped with elements that create missing electrons (holes) in its structure. This type of doping is called? (A) n-type doping (B) p-type doping (C) intrinsic doping (D) ionic doping

At the resonant frequency of an LCR circuit, the inductive and capacitive reactances cancel each other, resulting in minimum impedance. This condition is known as? (A) Maximum resonance (B) Pure resistance (C) Zero reactance (D) Phase shift

Light from distant galaxies appears shifted toward longer wavelengths due to the expansion of space itself. This observed effect is known as? (A) Blue shift (B) Doppler effect (C) Redshift (D) Interference

A liquid surface behaves like a stretched elastic membrane because molecules at the surface experience unequal forces compared to those inside. This effect is called? (A) Viscosity (B) Surface tension (C) Buoyancy (D) Adhesion

An object immersed in water experiences an upward force that reduces its apparent weight. This force arises due to pressure differences and is known as? (A) Gravitational force (B) Buoyant force (C) Magnetic force (D) Normal force

When work is done against friction or resistance, part of mechanical energy is converted into heat, reducing useful energy. This conversion is explained by which principle? (A) Energy conservation (B) Energy dissipation (C) Momentum conservation (D) Elasticity

When a gas is heated, its molecules move faster, increasing the average separation between them, which leads to a decrease in density. This behavior is explained by? (A) Boyle’s law (B) Thermal expansion (C) Hooke’s law (D) Ohm’s law
In nuclear reactions, a small amount of mass is converted into a large amount of energy according to a famous equation. This relationship is given by? (A) F=ma (B) V=IR (C) E=mc^2 (D) PV=nRT

sciFi.question: In Star Wars, what material are lightsaber blades made of?
sciFi.answer: Plasma (contained within a magnetic field created by a kyber crystal)
sciFi.hint: The colour depends on the alignment of the crystal.

sciFi.question:
In 'Interstellar', the 'Slingshot' maneuver around a black hole is a real orbital mechanic. To gain velocity without using fuel, what property of the massive body is the spacecraft 'stealing'?
(A) Angular Momentum
(B) Thermal Radiation
(C) Magnetic Flux
(D) Hawking Radiation
(E) Atomic Mass

In 'Ant-Man', the 'Quantum Realm' depicts a 'Probability Cloud'. In the Schrodinger Equation, what does the square of the wavefunction ($|\psi|^2$) represent?
(A) The exact velocity of Scott Lang
(B) The probability density of finding a particle in a specific location
(C) The total energy of the suit
(D) The rate of time dilation
(E) The temperature of the subatomic space
sciFi.answer: The probability density of finding a particle in a specific location

sciFi.question:
In 'The Marvels', heroes swap places instantaneously. If this were 'Quantum Teleportation', what must be exchanged between the two locations first to allow the transfer of the quantum state?
(A) A stream of liquid nitrogen
(B) A classical bit of information and a pre-entangled pair
(C) A high-intensity laser beam
(D) A gravitational wave
(E) A magnetic monopole
sciFi.answer: A classical bit of information and a pre-entangled pair

sciFi.question:
In 'Inception', the 'Penrose Stairs' represent an impossible object. In topology, if a dream architect creates a surface with only one side and one edge, they have constructed a:
(A) Klein Bottle
(B) Mobius Strip
(C) Euclidean Plane
(D) Hyperbolic Paraboloid
(E) Riemann Surface

In 'Arrival', the Heptapods' ship defies 'Aerodynamic Lift'. If the ship stays aloft without moving air, it is likely generating a field that cancels which fundamental force carrier?
(A) Gluon
(B) Photon
(C) Graviton
(D) W-Boson
(E) Z-Boson

In 'The Matrix', the 'Simulation Hypothesis' suggests reality is mathematical. If the universe has a minimum possible length (like a pixel), it refers to the:
(A) Hubble Length
(B) Planck Length
(C) Fermi Constant
(D) Bohr Radius
(E) Schwarzschild Radius

In 'The Martian', Watney uses 'Electrolysis' to produce Oxygen. This process involves using electricity to break the chemical bonds of which molecule?
(A) Carbon Dioxide
(B) Methane
(C) Water ($H_2O$)
(D) Ammonia
(E) Nitrogen

In 'Interstellar', 'Spaghettification' occurs near the singularity. This effect is caused by 'Tidal Forces,' which represent the difference in:
(A) Atmospheric pressure between head and feet
(B) Gravitational pull between two points on an object
(C) Light speed in a vacuum
(D) Magnetic polarity
(E) Time flow in different galaxies
sciFi.answer: Gravitational pull between two points on an object

sciFi.question:
In 'Ant-Man', 'Quantum Tunneling' allows particles to pass through barriers. This happens because a particle's wavefunction:
(A) Turns into a solid
(B) Has a non-zero probability of existing on the other side of the barrier
(C) Moves faster than light
(D) Becomes a wave of sound
(E) Shrinks to zero mass
sciFi.answer: Has a non-zero probability of existing on the other side of the barrier

sciFi.question:
In 'The Marvels', the 'Singularity' created by the bracelets is a 'Kerr Black Hole'. Unlike a standard black hole, a Kerr black hole possesses:
(A) Electric charge only
(B) Angular momentum (Spin)
(C) No event horizon
(D) Infinite life span
(E) A square shape

In 'Interstellar', the 'Causal Loop' involves Cooper sending data to his past self. This is theoretically possible via a 'Closed Timelike Curve' (CTC). Which theory of physics allows for the existence of CTCs?
(A) Special Relativity
(B) General Relativity
(C) Quantum Chromodynamics
(D) Fluid Dynamics
(E) Thermodynamics

In 'Arrival', the aliens' propulsion involves 'Inertial Mass Manipulation'. If you reduce an object's inertial mass to zero, how much force is required to accelerate it to the speed of light?
(A) Infinite Force
(B) Zero Force
(C) 100 Newtons
(D) Force equal to its weight
(E) It cannot be accelerated

In 'The Matrix', the 'Bullet Time' effect mimics 'Relativistic Doppler Shift'. If Neo moves toward a blue light at near-light speed, the frequency he perceives will:
(A) Increase further into the ultraviolet
(B) Decrease into the red
(C) Stay the same
(D) Turn into a sound wave
(E) Vanish completely

In 'Ant-Man', Scott is warned about 'Quantum Entanglement' with Janet. If two particles are entangled, measuring the spin of one 'instantly' determines the other. This phenomenon was famously called 'Spooky action at a distance' by:
(A) Richard Feynman
(B) Albert Einstein
(C) Niels Bohr
(D) Stephen Hawking
(E) Werner Heisenberg

In 'The Martian', to survive the vacuum of space during the final rescue, Watney punctures his suit. This uses the 'Conservation of Momentum'. If he ejects gas in one direction, his body moves:
(A) In the same direction
(B) In the opposite direction
(C) Upward only
(D) It doesn't move
(E) He starts spinning in place

In 'Interstellar', an observer on the Endurance watches a clock on Miller's Planet. If the planet clock appears to tick once every 7 years, what specific shift in the electromagnetic spectrum would the light from that clock exhibit?
(A) A massive displacement toward shorter wavelengths
(B) A compression of the wave period
(C) A significant redshift toward the infrared or radio spectrum
(D) A complete inversion of the photon's spin
(E) No spectral shift, only a change in brightness
sciFi.answer: A significant redshift toward the infrared or radio spectrum

sciFi.question:
In 'Arrival', the Heptapods' perception of 'Simultaneity' implies that the universe is a fixed 4D construct. If this is true, which concept of classical mechanics becomes an observer-dependent illusion?
(A) Mass
(B) Momentum
(C) The Arrow of Entropy
(D) Electric Charge
(E) Angular Velocity

Ant-Man shrinks to 10 nanometers but retains his 80kg mass. Given that the 'Strong Nuclear Force' has a range of approximately 1 femtometer, what would happen if he accidentally compressed his own atoms further into that range?
(A) He would become a liquid
(B) He would trigger a localized runaway fusion event
(C) He would lose all electrical charge
(D) He would drift away due to solar winds
(E) His density would drop to zero
sciFi.answer: He would trigger a localized runaway fusion event

sciFi.question:
In 'Inception', the 'kick' utilizes the vestibular system's reaction to gravity. If a dreamer is in a state of freefall in reality, why does the dream world lose its consistent 'down' vector?
(A) The dreamer's brain stops producing dopamine
(B) The lack of a normal force on the body removes the reference point for perceived gravity
(C) The subconscious mind cannot process acceleration
(D) Dreams only exist in zero-G
(E) Friction increases to infinity
sciFi.answer: The lack of a normal force on the body removes the reference point for perceived gravity

sciFi.question:
In 'The Matrix', the machines harvest heat from humans. Based on the Carnot Efficiency limit, why is using a biological organism as a primary thermal power source fundamentally flawed?
(A) Humans are too cold to create a high-temperature gradient
(B) Humans produce AC current
(C) Biological tissue is a perfect insulator
(D) The 'waste' heat is actually recycled into thoughts
(E) Machines prefer solar energy anyway
sciFi.answer: Humans are too cold to create a high-temperature gradient

sciFi.question:
In 'The Martian', Watney uses Hydrazine ($N_2H_4$) to produce water. If he accidentally allowed the pure Hydrogen to mix with the Habitat's Oxygen without a controlled spark, what is the most likely outcome?
(A) The room would fill with breathable steam
(B) An immediate, high-velocity combustion or explosion
(C) The creation of liquid Nitrogen
(D) The internal pressure would drop to zero
(E) The potatoes would grow 10x faster
sciFi.answer: An immediate, high-velocity combustion or explosion

sciFi.question:
In 'The Marvels', the 'Entanglement' swap is instantaneous. If this follows the 'No-Communication Theorem' of real physics, why is it impossible to send a text message using this method?
(A) The speed of light is too slow
(B) Quantum states are random and cannot be 'steered' to carry specific data
(C) The heroes don't have phones
(D) Space-time is too thick
(E) Gravity blocks the signal
sciFi.answer: Quantum states are random and cannot be 'steered' to carry specific data

sciFi.question:
If Ant-Man maintains his mass but shrinks to the size of a tardigrade, he would likely suffocate even with an oxygen tank. Why?
(A) The air molecules are too large to fit in his nose
(B) His metabolism would accelerate so much he would burn through his O2 in seconds
(C) Surface tension would seal his mouth shut
(D) The tank would become too heavy to lift
(E) Nitrogen becomes toxic at small scales
sciFi.answer: His metabolism would accelerate so much he would burn through his O2 in seconds

sciFi.question:
In 'Interstellar', the Tesseract represents a 5th-dimensional 'Bulk'. If a 5D being observes a 4D human timeline, how would a person's life appear to them?
(A) As a single, solid, winding physical filament or 'worm' through space
(B) As a series of flickering lights
(C) As a 2D photograph
(D) As a sphere of water
(E) As a sound wave
sciFi.answer: As a single, solid, winding physical filament or 'worm' through space

sciFi.question:
In 'Arrival', the Heptapod logograms are 'Semasiographic'. Unlike human alphabets, what does this imply about the relationship between the written symbol and the spoken sound?
(A) The symbol and sound are perfectly linked
(B) There is no inherent link; the symbol represents a concept directly
(C) The sound is higher pitch than the symbol
(D) The symbols can only be read in the dark
(E) Only machines can read them
sciFi.answer: There is no inherent link; the symbol represents a concept directly

sciFi.question:
In 'The Matrix', Neo learns skills via direct brain upload. The specialized cells that transmit these electrical signals are:
(A) Arteries
(B) Neurons
(C) Platelets
(D) Hormones
(E) T-cells

In 'The Marvels', a wormhole 'folds' space. To fold a 3D space, you must mathematically move through a:
(A) 2D plane
(B) 3D box
(C) 4D Bulk/Hyper-dimension
(D) Vacuum
(E) Magnet

In 'Inception', recursion is shown via infinite loop architecture. This is a 3D representation of:
(A) Addition
(B) Self-reference/Looping
(C) Subtraction
(D) Prime numbers
(E) Geometry

In 'Arrival', sound travels through a denser atmosphere. Compared to Earth's air, sound waves move:
(A) Slower
(B) Faster
(C) At the same speed
(D) Backwards
(E) Not at all

In 'Interstellar', a 'Singularity' at the center of a black hole is a theoretical point of:
(A) Infinite Volume
(B) Infinite Density
(C) Zero Mass
(D) Absolute Zero
(E) Infinite Light

In 'The Martian', Watney must wash Martian soil to remove which toxic substance before planting?
(A) Dust
(B) Perchlorates
(C) Gold
(D) Oxygen
(E) Ice

In 'Ant-Man', subatomic particles like Quarks are held together by which fundamental force?
(A) Gravity
(B) Strong Nuclear Force
(C) Weak Nuclear Force
(D) Magnetism
(E) Friction

In 'The Matrix', the 'Allegory of the Cave' philosophy suggests that:
(A) Reality is a cave
(B) Most people perceive shadows as reality
(C) Fighting dragons is easy
(D) Coding is fun
(E) Computers are the sun
sciFi.answer: Most people perceive shadows as reality

sciFi.question:
In 'The Marvels', when Monica phases through a solid wall, she is mimicking the quantum phenomenon of:
(A) Tunneling
(B) Entanglement
(C) Decoupling
(D) Fusion
(E) Fission

In 'Interstellar', TARS has an 'Honesty' setting to make him more human-like. This refers to the ability to pass the:
(A) Blood test
(B) Turing Test
(C) Vision test
(D) Stress test
(E) IQ test

In 'Interstellar', if Miller's Planet transmits a light signal every 7 Earth years, what specific shift in frequency occurs for the observer?
(A) Blueshift
(B) Gravitational Redshift
(C) Total Reflection
(D) Phase Inversion
(E) No Shift

In 'Arrival', Louise is a 'Non-causal' agent because she knows the future. This concept challenges the:
(A) First Law of Motion
(B) Second Law of Thermodynamics
(C) Ideal Gas Law
(D) Law of Reflection
(E) Static Equilibrium

Ant-Man shrinks to 10 nanometers, smaller than the wavelength of visible light. To a human observer, he would appear:
(A) Bright red
(B) Invisible/Black
(C) Transparent
(D) Glowing blue
(E) Giant

In 'Inception', if time dilation is 1:20 per dream level, how much real-time passes during 10 years in the 4th level?
(A) 1 hour
(B) 2.25 seconds
(C) 1 minute
(D) 1 day
(E) 5 hours

In 'The Matrix', if humans were truly used for energy, the system would fail because they consume more energy than they produce. This is a violation of:
(A) Ohm’s Law
(B) Thermal Efficiency
(C) Gravity
(D) Sound speed
(E) Magnetic flux

In 'The Marvels', swap speeds are determined by momentum. If a 60kg hero swaps with an 80kg hero at 10m/s, for momentum to be conserved, the new velocity must:
(A) Increase
(B) Decrease
(C) Stay the same
(D) Stop completely
(E) Reverse

In 'Interstellar', a Tesseract is a 4D hypercube. If a 3D sphere looks like a growing circle in 2D, what does a 4D sphere look like in 3D?
(A) A static cube
(B) A sphere that grows and then shrinks
(C) A flat line
(D) A pyramid
(E) A point
sciFi.answer: A sphere that grows and then shrinks

sciFi.question:
In 'The Martian', Watney burns hydrazine for water. Where does the Hydrogen for the reaction come from?
(A) Martian atmosphere
(B) The chemical bonds of the fuel
(C) The soil
(D) His breath
(E) The potatoes
sciFi.answer: The chemical bonds of the fuel

sciFi.question:
In 'Arrival', aliens use 'Non-zero-sum' logic. In game theory, this implies:
(A) Total loss
(B) Mutual benefit is possible
(C) Only one winner
(D) No game
(E) Infinite points

In 'Interstellar', binary code (0 and 1) is transmitted. What is the decimal value of the binary string 1011?
(A) 5
(B) 10
(C) 11
(D) 13
(E) 15

In 'The Matrix', the 'Oracle' can see the future of the simulation. If the simulation is 'Deterministic,' it means that:
(A) Every event is determined by preceding causes and laws
(B) Everything happens by random chance
(C) Humans have total free will
(D) The machines have no power
(E) The code is always broken
sciFi.answer: Every event is determined by preceding causes and laws

sciFi.question:
In 'The Marvels', the heroes switch by 'Quantum Entanglement'. If one hero's state is 'Up', the entangled hero's state must be:
(A) Also Up
(B) Down
(C) Left
(D) Right
(E) It remains unknown

In 'Inception', Cobb uses a spinning top. If the top experiences 'Precession,' it is a result of:
(A) Gravity and Angular Momentum
(B) Air resistance only
(C) The dreamer's fear
(D) Light reflecting off the surface
(E) Magnetic fields

In 'Ant-Man', the 'Wasp' uses bio-electric stings. This process of generating electricity within a living organism is known as:
(A) Piezoelectricity
(B) Electroluminescence
(C) Bioelectrogenesis
(D) Thermoelectricity
(E) Photovoltaics

In 'The Martian', the 'Gravity Assist' to return to Mars uses the Earth's orbital velocity. This is an application of:
(A) Newton's First Law
(B) Hooke's Law
(C) The Work-Energy Theorem
(D) Snell's Law
(E) Bernoulli's Principle

puzzles.question: A prisoner stands before two doors: one to freedom, one to death. Two guards stand there; one always lies, one always tells the truth. You don't know who is who. You can ask only one guard one question to find the door to freedom. What do you ask?
puzzles.answer: Ask: “Which door would the other guard say leads to freedom? Then choose the opposite door

puzzles.question: There are 10 bags of gold coins. Nine bags contain 10g coins, but one bag contains 11g counterfeit coins. You have a digital scale that provides an exact weight. What is the minimum number of weighings needed to identify the counterfeit bag?

A digital safe requires a 4-digit code. The clues are: The first is double the fourth. The second is the sum of the first and fourth. The third is the second minus five. No digits are zero. What is the code?

Four people (A, B, C, D) must cross a bridge at night with one torch. Speeds are 1, 2, 5, and 10 minutes. Only two can cross at once at the slower person's pace. How do they all cross in exactly 17 minutes?

Three boxes are labeled 'Apples', 'Oranges', and 'Apples & Oranges'. Every label is currently incorrect. You can pick only one fruit from one box to relabel all three correctly. Which box do you pick from?
A man is found hanging in an empty, locked room with a puddle of water under him. There is no furniture. How did he reach the ceiling to tie the rope?
A spy watches a guard. Guard says 'Twelve', officer says 'Six' (let in). Guard says 'Six', officer says 'Three' (let in). Guard says 'Ten', spy says 'Five' and is shot. What should he have said?
Outside a sealed room are three switches. One controls a bulb inside. You can flip switches as you wish but only enter once. If the bulb is off when you enter, how do you know the correct switch?
A king has three advisors in hats (Red or Blue). At least one is Red. They see others' hats but not their own. After a long silence, one realizes his is Red. How did he know?
puzzles.answer: Their silence means neither saw two Blues → so at least one of them is Red → hence he deduces his own hat is Red

puzzles.question: You have 1,017 chips in a dark room. Exactly 23 are black-side up. How can you split them into two piles so both have the same number of black-side up chips without seeing them?

100 prisoners each have a box numbered 1–100 containing a random permutation of numbers 1–100. Each prisoner may open 50 boxes. They can agree on a strategy beforehand. What is the optimal strategy to maximize survival probability?
puzzles.answer: Start at your number and follow the permutation cycle.

puzzles.question: You have two identical ropes that each burn in exactly 60 minutes but not at a uniform rate. How do you measure exactly 45 minutes?
A 3L and 5L jug are available with unlimited water. Measure exactly 4L with minimum steps.
puzzles.answer: Fill 5L , Pour into 3L → (2L left in 5L) , Empty 3L, Pour 2L into 3L , Fill 5L again , Pour into 3L (needs 1L)

puzzles.question: You have 12 identical-looking balls, one is heavier or lighter. Using a balance scale only 3 times, how do you find the odd ball and its type?
A submarine is floating in a small, enclosed swimming pool. It ejects a heavy iron anchor into the water. Does the water level of the pool rise, fall, or stay the same?
You have hot black coffee and cold milk. You must wait five minutes before drinking. To have the coldest coffee possible after the wait, should you add milk immediately or just before drinking?
A fly is at the bottom of a sealed glass jar on a sensitive digital scale. The fly suddenly takes off and hovers. What happens to the weight reading at the moment of takeoff?
Why are manhole covers engineered to be round rather than square in almost every major city?
puzzles.answer: If we take a sqaure manhole it may fall diagonally through the hole which is not in case of circular one

puzzles.question: An electric train travels North at 100 mph. The wind blows West at 50 mph. In which direction does the smoke from the engine travel?
